Beginner.question: What is Machine Learning mainly about?

options.A: Learning useful patterns from data
options.B: Writing every rule manually

Beginner.question: In machine learning, what are features used for?

options.A: They are input variables used by the model
options.B: They are only the final answers

Beginner.question: What makes supervised learning different from unsupervised learning?

options.A: Supervised learning uses known target values during training
options.B: Supervised learning never uses data
options.C: Supervised learning only groups data without labels

Beginner.question: Which task is an example of supervised learning?

options.A: Predicting customer churn using labeled examples
options.B: Finding hidden groups without a target variable

options.A: A problem where the goal is to predict a numeric outcome
options.B: A problem where the goal is only to find hidden groups
options.C: A problem where the output must always be yes or no

Beginner.question: Which example is a classification problem?

options.A: Predicting whether a review is positive or negative
options.B: Predicting the exact sale price of a house
options.C: Reducing the number of variables in a dataset

Beginner.question: What is the goal of unsupervised learning?

options.A: Discovering hidden structure, patterns, or groups in data
options.B: Predicting a known target value

Beginner.question: Why is data preparation important before applying machine learning?

options.A: Clean and prepared data helps the model learn more reliable patterns
options.B: It removes the need for training data
options.C: It makes every model perfect automatically

Intermediate.question: What is the purpose of splitting data into training and testing sets?

options.A: To train the model on one part and evaluate it on unseen data
options.B: To remove all target values from the data
options.C: To make the dataset smaller only

Intermediate.question: When is stratified sampling especially useful?

options.A: When target classes are imbalanced
options.B: When the data has no features
options.C: When the model does not need evaluation

Intermediate.question: What is k-fold cross validation used for?

options.A: Estimating model performance by training and validating on different parts of the data
options.B: Converting categorical values into images
options.C: Removing all observations from the dataset

Intermediate.question: What does high bias usually indicate?

options.A: The model is too simple and may underfit the data
options.B: The model has memorized every noise pattern
options.C: The model has no input variables

Intermediate.question: What does high variance usually indicate?

options.A: The model is too sensitive to training data and may overfit
options.B: The model is too simple to learn any pattern

options.A: It checks how well the model performs and whether it may generalize to new data
options.B: It guarantees that the model will never fail
options.C: It replaces the need for training

options.A: Preparing and transforming input variables so models can learn effectively
options.B: Choosing the physical computer used for training
options.C: Deleting the target column before supervised learning

Intermediate.question: What does logistic regression estimate in classification?

options.A: The probability that an observation belongs to a class
options.B: Only a continuous numeric price
options.C: The number of rows in the dataset

Intermediate.question: What does the machine learning modeling process usually include?

options.A: Preparing data, splitting it, training a model, evaluating it, and improving performance
options.B: Only writing fixed rules manually without data
options.C: Deleting the testing data before evaluation

Intermediate.question: What is linear regression commonly used for?

options.A: Predicting a numeric output using a linear relationship with input variables
options.B: Grouping unlabeled observations without a target value
options.C: Choosing one class from image pixels only

Advanced.question: What is the main purpose of regularized regression?

options.A: To control model complexity and reduce overfitting
options.B: To remove all predictors from the model
options.C: To make the model memorize every training example

Advanced.question: What can lasso regression do to some coefficients?

options.A: Shrink some coefficients to zero
options.B: Convert all targets into images

Advanced.question: How do decision trees make predictions?

options.A: By splitting the data into smaller groups based on feature values
options.B: By using only random guesses

Advanced.question: Why can very deep decision trees be risky?

options.A: They may overfit the data
options.B: They cannot be used for classification
options.C: They do not use decision rules

Advanced.question: How do random forests improve over a single decision tree?

options.A: They combine many trees to reduce instability and improve generalization
options.B: They use only one fixed rule

Advanced.question: What is the main idea of gradient boosting?

options.A: Building models sequentially so each new model corrects previous errors
options.B: Training many independent trees without using errors

Advanced.question: What does deep learning use to learn complex patterns?

options.A: Neural networks with multiple layers
options.B: Only one manually written rule

Advanced.question: What is the purpose of interpretable machine learning?

options.A: Understanding how models make predictions
options.B: Making models impossible to explain

Beginner.question: What is Deep Learning mainly based on?

options.A: Neural networks with multiple layers
options.B: Writing all rules manually

Beginner.question: Why is Deep Learning useful for complex inputs such as images and sound?

options.A: It can learn meaningful representations across several hidden layers
options.B: It removes the need for numeric data
options.C: It only works with one input feature

Beginner.question: In a basic neural network, what does the input layer do?

options.A: It receives the original features from the data
options.B: It produces the final prediction

Beginner.question: What is the role of hidden layers?

options.A: They transform input features into useful internal representations
options.B: They store only the final class labels
options.C: They remove all weights from the network

Beginner.question: What is the MNIST dataset commonly used for?

options.A: Teaching image classification of handwritten digits
options.B: Training models to predict house prices

Beginner.question: Why should pixel values often be divided by 255 for image data?

options.A: To scale values between 0 and 1
options.B: To delete all dark pixels

Beginner.question: What is one-hot encoding used for in multiclass targets?

options.A: Representing each class as a vector with the correct class marked as 1
options.B: Removing all classes except one

Beginner.question: What does the output layer produce?

options.A: The final prediction
options.B: The original raw dataset

Intermediate.question: What is a feedforward neural network?

options.A: A network where information moves from input through hidden layers to output
options.B: A network where information moves randomly in all directions

Intermediate.question: Which component allows a neural network to measure error and learn from it?

options.A: A feedback mechanism
options.B: A file name

options.A: The structure of layers, nodes, and activation functions
options.B: The physical location of the computer

Intermediate.question: What does increasing layers and nodes usually increase?

options.A: Model capacity and computational cost
options.B: The number of target labels only
options.C: The need to remove all features

options.A: A fully connected layer where nodes connect to nodes in the next layer
options.B: A layer that contains no connections
options.C: A layer used only to store images

Intermediate.question: Which activation function is often used in hidden layers because it passes positive signals and sets negative values to zero?

options.A: ReLU
options.B: Softmax

Intermediate.question: Which output activation is commonly used for multiclass classification?

options.A: Softmax
options.B: Linear activation

Intermediate.question: For regression problems in neural networks, what output layer design is commonly used?

options.A: One node with a linear activation
options.B: Ten softmax nodes for every regression problem

Intermediate.question: What is the main role of hidden layers in a neural network?

options.A: Learning intermediate representations between the input and output layers
options.B: Storing the final answer before training starts
options.C: Removing the need for activation functions

options.A: The process of working backward through layers to calculate how weights contributed to error
options.B: The process of deleting hidden layers
options.C: The process of converting images into folders

Advanced.question: What does a loss function measure?

options.A: How far predictions are from the correct answers
options.B: How many files are in a project
options.C: How many layers should be deleted

Advanced.question: Which loss function may be used for regression?

options.A: Mean squared error
options.B: Categorical cross entropy only

options.A: How the model updates its weights to reduce loss
options.B: How the dataset is stored on disk
options.C: How the input layer is removed

Advanced.question: What does batch size control during training?

options.A: How many observations are processed before a weight update
options.B: How many classes are deleted

Advanced.question: What does model capacity refer to?

options.A: How much complexity a neural network can learn
options.B: How much storage space the computer has

Advanced.question: What is the main purpose of dropout?

options.A: To reduce overfitting by randomly setting some layer outputs to zero during training
options.B: To remove the loss function
options.C: To make the network depend more strongly on one node

Advanced.question: What can happen if the learning rate is too large?

options.A: The model may fail to settle near a good solution
options.B: Training always becomes perfect
